# THIS FILE IS AUTOMATICALLY GENERATED BY CARGO
#
# When uploading crates to the registry Cargo will automatically
# "normalize" Cargo.toml files for maximal compatibility
# with all versions of Cargo and also rewrite `path` dependencies
# to registry (e.g., crates.io) dependencies.
#
# If you are reading this file be aware that the original Cargo.toml
# will likely look very different (and much more reasonable).
# See Cargo.toml.orig for the original contents.

[package]
edition = "2021"
name = "sqlx-sqlite"
version = "0.8.6"
authors = [
    "Ryan Leckey <leckey.ryan@gmail.com>",
    "Austin Bonander <austin.bonander@gmail.com>",
    "Chloe Ross <orangesnowfox@gmail.com>",
    "Daniel Akhterov <akhterovd@gmail.com>",

content.text:
The **only** change is widening the `libsqlite3-sys` version requirement from `= 0.30.1` to `>= 0.30.1, < 0.36.0`. The Rust source files are identical to upstream `sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6`.

This was vendored to resolve a version conflict: something else in the dependency tree (likely the `secret-store` crate or `matrix` encryption work added in that same commit) pulled in a newer `libsqlite3-sys` (e.g., 0.31+), but upstream `sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6` pins `libsqlite3-sys = 0.30.1` exactly. The patch relaxes that pin so both can coexist.

**It's safe to remove** if/when `sqlx-sqlite` releases a version that accepts the newer `libsqlite3-sys`. You can check with:

```bash
cargo update -p sqlx-sqlite  # see if a newer version resolves the conflict
```

Alternatively, if the dependency that needed a newer `libsqlite3-sys` is no longer present, the vendor patch is also unnecessary.

content.text:
The situation is clear. Here's the summary:

**The vendor patch is still necessary.** The conflict is:

- `matrix-sdk-sqlite` â `rusqlite 0.37` â `libsqlite3-sys 0.35.0`
- `sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6` (upstream) pins `libsqlite3-sys = 0.30.1` exactly

The vendored copy loosens that pin to `>= 0.30.1, < 0.36.0` so both can share `libsqlite3-sys 0.35.0`. The source code is otherwise **identical** to upstream â only `Cargo.toml` differs.

**Why it can't be removed yet:**
- Latest stable sqlx is still `0.8.6` â no newer release exists
- `0.9.0-alpha.1` exists but is pre-release
- The conflict comes from `matrix-sdk-sqlite` pulling in a newer `rusqlite`/`libsqlite3-sys`, which is inherent to the Matrix SDK dependency

**Options to eliminate the vendor eventually:**
1. Wait for `sqlx 0.9.0` stable (should accept newer `libsqlite3-sys`)
2. Pin `matrix-sdk` to a version using `rusqlite` â€ 0.32 / `libsqlite3-sys` 0.30.x (likely not available)
3. Keep the vendor patch (current approach â low risk since only the version bound changed)

For now, the patch is the pragmatic choice. I'd suggest adding a comment in the root `Cargo.toml` so future contributors understand:

```toml
[patch.crates-io]
# Widen libsqlite3-sys pin (0.30.1 â >=0.30.1,<0.36.0) so sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6
# can coexist with matrix-sdk-sqlite's rusqlite 0.37 (libsqlite3-sys 0.35).
# Remove once sqlx â„ 0.9 ships stable.
sqlx-sqlite = { path = "vendor/sqlx-sqlite" }
```

Want me to add that comment?

content.text:
Even better â a submodule of the full sqlx repo would be heavy. But there's a catch: we can't use a plain Cargo git dependency either, because upstream `sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6` pins `libsqlite3-sys = 0.30.1` exactly, and that's the whole reason for the vendor patch.

So a `git = "..."` reference like:
```toml
[patch.crates-io]
sqlx-sqlite = { git = "https://github.com/launchbadge/sqlx", rev = "bab1b02..." }
```
would pull the **unmodified** Cargo.toml and the `libsqlite3-sys` version conflict would return.

The options are:

1. **Fork sqlx** on your GitHub, apply the one-line version bound change, and reference that fork via `git + rev` in `[patch.crates-io]` â no vendor dir, no submodule, cleanest solution
2. **Submodule + symlink** â add upstream sqlx as a submodule, symlink `vendor/sqlx-sqlite/src` â submodule, keep only the modified `Cargo.toml` in the repo
3. **Keep the vendor** as-is (current state)

Option 1 is the least pain. Want me to go with the fork approach? If so, which GitHub org/user should I fork to â `openclaw`?

[package]
name = "sqlx-sqlite"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/sqlx"
description = "SQLite driver implementation for SQLx. Not for direct use; see the `sqlx` crate for details."
version.workspace = true
license.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
authors.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true

# See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html

[features]
any = ["sqlx-core/any"]
json = ["sqlx-core/json", "serde"]
offline = ["sqlx-core/offline", "serde"]
migrate = ["sqlx-core/migrate"]

# Type integrations
chrono = ["dep:chrono", "sqlx-core/chrono"]
time = ["dep:time", "sqlx-core/time"]
uuid = ["dep:uuid", "sqlx-core/uuid"]

regexp = ["dep:regex"]

preupdate-hook = ["libsqlite3-sys/preupdate_hook"]

bundled = ["libsqlite3-sys/bundled"]
unbundled = ["libsqlite3-sys/buildtime_bindgen"]

[dependencies]
futures-core = { version = "0.3.19", default-features = false }
futures-channel = { version = "0.3.19", default-features = false, features = ["sink", "alloc", "std"] }
# used by the SQLite worker thread to block on the async mutex that locks the database handle
futures-executor = { version = "0.3.19" }
futures-intrusive = "0.5.0"
futures-util = { version = "0.3.19", default-features = false, features = ["alloc", "sink"] }

chrono = { workspace = true, optional = true }
time = { workspace = true, optional = true }
uuid = { workspace = true, optional = true }

url = { version = "2.2.2" }
percent-encoding = "2.1.0"
serde_urlencoded = "0.7"

flume = { version = "0.11.0", default-features = false, features = ["async"] }

atoi = "2.0"

log = "0.4.18"
tracing = { version = "0.1.37", features = ["log"] }
thiserror = "2.0.0"

serde = { version = "1.0.145", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
regex = { version = "1.5.5", optional = true }

[dependencies.libsqlite3-sys]
version = "0.30.1"
default-features = false
features = [
    "pkg-config",
    "vcpkg",
    "unlock_notify"
]

[dependencies.sqlx-core]
workspace = true

[dev-dependencies]
sqlx = { workspace = true, default-features = false, features = ["macros", "runtime-tokio", "tls-none", "sqlite"] }

[lints]
workspace = true

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
features = ["bundled", "any", "json", "chrono", "time", "uuid"]

cd /tmp/sqlx-fork && git add sqlx-sqlite/Cargo.toml && git commit -m "fix: widen libsqlite3-sys version bound for rusqlite 0.37 compat

sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6 pins libsqlite3-sys = 0.30.1 exactly, which conflicts
with matrix-sdk-sqlite -> rusqlite 0.37 -> libsqlite3-sys 0.35.

Widen the bound to >=0.30.1, <0.36.0 so both can coexist." 2>&1

# Widen libsqlite3-sys pin (0.30.1 â >=0.30.1,<0.36.0) so sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6

content.text:
-10,550 lines deleted. Here's what was done:

- **Forked** `launchbadge/sqlx` â `moltis-org/sqlx`
- **Created branch** `moltis/widen-libsqlite3-sys` from `v0.8.6` with the one-line `libsqlite3-sys` version bound fix
- **Replaced** the `[patch.crates-io]` path reference with a `git + rev` reference to the fork
- **Also patched** `sqlx-core` from the same rev (needed so `sqlx-macros-core` sees consistent types)
- **Removed** the entire `vendor/sqlx-sqlite/` directory (~10.5K lines)
- No CI changes needed â Cargo fetches git dependencies automatically

The final `Cargo.toml` patch section:
```toml
[patch.crates-io]
# Widen libsqlite3-sys pin (0.30.1 â >=0.30.1,<0.36.0) so sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6
# can coexist with matrix-sdk-sqlite's rusqlite 0.37 (libsqlite3-sys 0.35).
# Remove once sqlx â„ 0.9 ships stable.
sqlx-sqlite = { git = "https://github.com/moltis-org/sqlx", rev = "33747ad2..." }
sqlx-core = { git = "https://github.com/moltis-org/sqlx", rev = "33747ad2..." }
```

Ready to commit when you are.

refactor: replace vendored sqlx-sqlite with git dependency

matrix-sdk-sqlite's rusqlite 0.37 (libsqlite3-sys 0.35).

Remove once sqlx >= 0.9 ships stable.
EOF
)"

content.text: The test mock provides `username: "@alice:matrix.org"` but the assertion checks for `@penso:matrix.org`. Let me look at the rendering code to understand the sigil logic, then fix the test.

Now I see the rendering logic at line 516:

```js
var usernameLabel = s.username ? (String(s.username).startsWith("@") ? s.username : `@${s.username}`) : "â";
```

So if `username` is `"@alice:matrix.org"` (starts with `@`), it renders as-is: `@alice:matrix.org`. The test mock gives `username: "@alice:matrix.org"`, so the UI will show `@alice:matrix.org`.

The test asserts `@penso:matrix.org` â simply the wrong username. The fix is straightforward:

await expect(page.getByText("@alice:matrix.org", { exact: true })).toBeVisible();
		await expect(page.getByText("@@alice:matrix.org", { exact: true })).toHaveCount(0);
